regime_justification: MOF executed suspected intervention after USDJPY breached 160; yen now at 156.50 but spread still wide, BOJ held at 0.75%, vol subdued, Tokyo CPI cooled to 1.5%—pressure building but unwind not yet forced.

domino_stage.label: Pre-Intervention
domino_stage.justification: USDJPY breached 160 threshold and MOF intervened on April 30 (~$35bn suspected); pair now at 156.50; BOJ held rate at 0.75% on April 28 with 3 dissents for hike; JGB 10Y at 2.50% (1997 high); risk reversals and basis data unavailable but volatility muted—classic pre-unwind fragility.

1w.label: Likely
1w.driver: Fresh MOF intervention and 156.50 level fragile; BOJ hold with dissents signals policy tension; next intervention round or renewed USDJPY rally could trigger vol spike.

2w.label: Likely
2w.driver: June 16-17 BOJ meeting inside window; market pricing hike risk; intervention effect may fade; if USDJPY retraces toward 160, second wave of unwind pressure builds.

3w.label: Watchful
3w.driver: Post-BOJ June decision clarity reduces ambiguity; if hike delivered, unwind risk eases; if hold, carry re-inflates; applied unwind prior—data alone would grade D.

4w.label: Watchful
4w.driver: Resolution scenario dominates: BOJ path clearer, intervention fatigue or success determines whether carry trade stabilizes or collapses; still fragile but less imminent.

bottom_line: Stage 2: MOF intervened April 30 after USDJPY broke 160; now at 156.50 with JGB 10Y at 2.50% (1997 high). BOJ held at 0.75% April 28 but 3 of 9 dissented for hike—normalization pressure rising. Spread 187 bps, vol low (implied vol data unavailable), Tokyo CPI cooled to 1.5%. Grades B/B: intervention fragile, June BOJ meeting (16-17) inside 2-week window is key catalyst. Crypto liquidity stable (BTC ~$77k) but sympathy drain likely if carry unwinds. Watch: second intervention round, USDJPY retracement to 158-160, BOJ June hike, or oil spike (WTI ~$101/bbl post-Iran deal rumors).

72.07 today; well below 100; rates vol subdued despite Fed dissents and intervention—regime change not yet triggered

trigger_map.note: Data unavailable but vol muted; intervention suggests positioning cautious; flip would signal unwind acceleration

narrative.p1: Overnight: MOF intervened April 30 after USDJPY breached 160, suspected ~$35bn yen-buying per BOJ data; pair now 156.50 (-1.56% from 159.82). Tokyo CPI ex-food April 1.5%, lowest since March 2022, undershooting BOJ 2% target on childcare subsidies. WTI crude dropped 3.82% to $101 on Iran peace proposal rumors (Pakistan confirmed delivery to US). Nikkei declined 1.06% on oil cost pressure. Stage change: 1 → 2 (Pre-Intervention) as MOF action confirms watch-line breach and policy tension.
narrative.p2: Quant anchors: US-JP spread 189 bps (4.39% - 2.50%), still wide but compressed 2 bps post-intervention. Carry-to-vol proxy unavailable (1M implied vol data missing). USDJPY 5-day pace: -1.56% (intervention-driven); 60-day mean: ~158.5, so current 156.50 is 2 yen compressed—stretched to downside but fragile. JGB 10Y 2.50% = 1997 high; BOJ held 0.75% April 28 but 3/9 dissented for hike, inflation forecast raised to 2.8% FY2026, growth cut to 0.5%. Domino Stage 2: intervention executed but vol muted (VIX 17.10, MOVE 72.07)—classic pre-unwind fragility. Grade B 1-2w: intervention effect may fade, June BOJ (16-17) inside window, USDJPY retracement to 158-160 would trigger second wave. Applied unwind prior at 3w: data ambiguous (BOJ June decision coin-flip), lean C vs. D; post-meeting clarity reduces tail risk but carry re-inflation possible if no hike.
narrative.p3: Crypto liquidity: BTC $77,062 (+2.00% 24h), XRP unavailable but likely tracking. Risk-on holding post-intervention; DXY 97.95 (-0.11%), stable. BTC correlation to risk assets suggests sympathy drain if carry unwinds: levered yen shorts liquidating would pull liquidity across crypto/equity/credit. Open interest direction and BTC-DXY 30d corr unavailable but historical pattern: carry unwind → crypto flush (Aug 2024 analog). Positioning implication: BTC $76-77k floor stable but vulnerable to Vol spike or second MOF action; if USDJPY rallies back to 158-160 and triggers intervention round 2, expect BTC drawdown to $72-74k range on risk-off cascade. Watch BTC vol (realized vs. implied) for early warning; muted vol now = complacency, not safety.

horizon_rationale.w1_w2: Week 1 fragile: MOF intervention effect fades historically within days-to-week; USDJPY at 156.50 but carry spread 189 bps still incentivizes reflation. Week 2 escalates: June 16-17 BOJ meeting inside window; 3 dissents April 28 + inflation forecast raise = hike risk elevated. If USDJPY retraces to 158-160 before June meeting, second intervention round likely, triggering vol spike and early unwind signatures. Grade B both weeks: data-driven, high catalyst density.
horizon_rationale.w2_w3: Week 2 ends with BOJ decision June 17; Week 3 absorbs reaction. If hike delivered (25 bps to 1.00%), spread narrows to ~139 bps, carry less attractive, unwind risk moderates to C. If hold, carry re-inflates, USDJPY rallies back toward 160, intervention fatigue question emerges—could flip to B if MOF credibility fades. Ambiguity in Week 3 resolved by BOJ action; applied unwind prior as tiebreaker (would grade D on hold scenario without prior, but thesis says complacency mispriced).
horizon_rationale.w3_w4: Week 3-4: scenario-weighted. Post-BOJ clarity reduces near-term catalyst density. If hike + intervention success, carry unwind risk eases to C-D as new equilibrium found (spread ~140 bps, USDJPY 150-155 range). If hold + intervention failure (USDJPY back to 160), unwind risk persists at C but timing pushes beyond 4-week horizon—MOF/BOJ coordination question becomes dominant variable. Oil wildcard: if Hormuz tensions reignite (WTI back to $120+, ¥/bbl >¥18,500), imported inflation forces BOJ hand, accelerates normalization, grades flip back to B.

watch_list: USDJPY retracement to 158-160 (second intervention trigger within 3-5 days)
BOJ June 16-17 decision: hike to 1.00% vs. hold at 0.75% (inside 2-week window)
Iran-US peace deal progress: WTI stability vs. Hormuz re-escalation (oil >$120 = ¥-inflation spike)
Tokyo CPI May 23 release: rebound above 2% would pressure BOJ, reinforce hike case
BTC vol expansion or flush below $75k (sympathy drain signal for cross-asset unwind)
